title: 10 best herbal decoctions for shortness of breath
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for shortness of breath are liquid extracts made by steeping herbs in hot water, which can provide relief from respiratory distress. These decoctions work by soothing and relaxing the airways, reducing inflammation and congestion, and promoting easier breathing. For example, peppermint, eucalyptus, and ginger decoctions have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help ease bronchial constriction, while licorice root and slippery elm decoctions soothe irritated lungs. By improving airflow and reducing discomfort, these herbal remedies can greatly improve the quality of life for individuals suffering from shortness of breath, enabling them to engage in daily activities with renewed confidence and energy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with shortness of breath because they contain bioactive compounds that help to relax and dilate the airways, making it easier for air to pass in and out. The soothing properties of licorice root also reduce inflammation and irritation in the lungs and bronchi, allowing for improved airflow and increased oxygen intake. Additionally, licorice decoctions have been shown to increase the production of surfactant, a natural lubricant that helps to prevent sticking and collapsing of the air sacs in the lungs, further reducing respiratory distress.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal licorice decoctions have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to shortness of breath by opening up constricted airways and making breathing easier.
Expectorant: Licorice root has expectorant properties that can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, which can help alleviate shortness of breath caused by congestion or coughing.

Antioxidant: Herbal licorice decoctions contain antioxidants that can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic breathing difficulties like COPD.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizic acid: This triterpenoid saponin helps with shortness of breath by reducing inflammation in the airways and lungs, making it easier to breathe.
Liquiritin: A flavonoid glycoside, liquiritin helps alleviate shortness of breath by acting as a bronchodilator, relaxing the airway muscles and increasing airflow.
18β-Glycyrrhetinic acid: This triterpenoid acid has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce congestion and inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound known to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate respiratory issues.
Leaves: Leaves are also used in decoctions, as they contain flavonoids and saponins that have bronchodilatory effects, helping to relieve shortness of breath.
Stems: Stems are used in some decoctions, as they contain similar compounds to the roots, including glycyrrhizin, which can help reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function.
remedy_recipe: Gather 5-10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root and clean it thoroughly with a soft brush.
Boil 1 liter of water in a saucepan and add the cleaned glycyrrhiza glabra root.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the decoction boil for 30-40 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the decoction to cool down to room temperature before serving or storing it in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower decoctions helps with shortness of breath because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to relax bronchial muscles, reduce airway constriction, and alleviate inflammation in the lungs. The decoction's expectorant properties also help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the herb's natural antispasmodic effects can help to calm coughing fits and soothe irritated throats, further reducing symptoms of shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower decoctions contain anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD).

Antioxidant activity: The decoction's antioxidant properties may help protect lung tissues from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of chronic respiratory diseases like COPD and pneumonia.

Bronchodilatory effects: Purple coneflower contains compounds that may help relax airway smooth muscle, increasing airflow and relieving bronchospasm, which can improve shortness of breath in conditions like asthma.
remedy_constituents: Isoquinoline alkaloids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath.
Flavonoids: As antioxidants, flavonoids can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to shortness of breath.

Cichoric acid: This caffeic acid derivative has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response to alleviate respiratory issues, including shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote lung health, making them a popular choice for treating shortness of breath.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea purpurea are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate respiratory issues and improve breathing.

Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea purpurea are used to make decoctions that help to soothe the respiratory tract, reduce inflammation, and improve lung function, making them a popular choice for treating shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30 grams of dried echinacea purpurea roots and 15 grams of dried flowers to prepare the decoction.
Crush the dried roots and flowers into smaller pieces using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Combine the crushed roots and flowers with 1 liter of boiling water in a large saucepan or pot.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 30 to 45 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme decoctions helps with shortness of breath because it has natural expectorant properties that loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. The antimicrobial compounds in thyme also help reduce inflammation and congestion in the lungs, allowing for more efficient oxygen exchange. Additionally, thyme's vasodilatory effects can help relax bronchial muscles, further improving airflow and alleviating shortness of breath symptoms. This natural remedy can provide relief from respiratory issues and promote overall lung health.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Thyme's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, reducing congestion and making it easier to breathe. This is particularly beneficial for individuals with respiratory issues such as bronchitis or chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD).
Antispasmodic properties: Thyme's antispasmodic properties can help relax the airways and reduce spasms that can constrict breathing, allowing for easier airflow and reduced shortness of breath.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can contribute to shortness of breath and other respiratory symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol acts as a bronchodilator, helping to open up airways and improve breathing in individuals with shortness of breath.
Caryophyllene oxide: Caryophyllene oxide is known to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate shortness of breath.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the lungs from oxidative damage and inflammation, thereby reducing the severity of shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of thymol, which has expectorant properties to help relieve respiratory issues.
Stems: Employed in decoctions for their ability to stimulate the nervous system and promote respiratory function.
Buds: Utilized in traditional medicine for their expectorant properties and ability to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 tablespoons of dried thymus vulgaris leaves and stems from a trusted herbal source.
Combine the dried thymus vulgaris with 1 quart of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes in a warm dark place to allow flavors to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.
Allow the decoction to cool completely before refrigerating or freezing for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butterfly milkweed decoctions helps with shortness of breath because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds that work together to dilate bronchial airways and reduce inflammation in the lungs. As a natural expectorant, it helps loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties soothe irritated lung tissues and help calm the body's response to allergens or irritants, providing welcome relief for individuals struggling with chronic shortness of breath.

Cardiac glycosides: Cardiac glycosides present in Milkweed may help regulate the heart's rhythm and increase cardiac output, improving blood circulation and oxygenation of tissues.

Flavonoids: The flavonoid content in Milkweed has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, contributing to improved breathing.

Asclepianone: This alkaloid constituent has shown potential in opening airways and reducing inflammation in the lungs, thereby helping to alleviate shortness of breath.

remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of iridoid glycosides, which are believed to have expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They are utilized for their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues like shortness of breath.
Flowers: They are used in decoctions due to their high concentration of saponins, which have expectorant properties that help relieve respiratory congestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-20 fresh roots of asclepias tuberosa and clean them thoroughly with water.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and measure out 5 grams for decoction.
Combine the chopped roots with 1 liter of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed decoctions helps with shortness of breath because it has a natural bronchodilatory effect, allowing for easier airflow in and out of the lungs. The decoction's active compounds, such as taraxasterol and beta-amyrin, work to relax airway muscles, reducing inflammation and constriction. This relaxation helps to open up the airways, making it easier to breathe deeply and comfortably, alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Bronchodilatory effect: Joe Pye Weed contains a compound called artemisinin, which has been shown to relax airway smooth muscle and improve lung function, helping to relieve shortness of breath.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, alleviating symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), which can contribute to shortness of breath.

Expectorant properties: Joe Pye Weed's expectorant compounds, including volatile oils and saponins, help loosen and expel mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing and wheezing.
remedy_constituents: Eupatorin: Eupatorin, a flavonoid glycoside, helps alleviate shortness of breath by inhibiting inflammatory responses and improving lung function.
Quercetin: Quercetin, a polyphenolic flavonoid, helps with shortness of breath by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can contribute to respiratory issues.
Kaempferol: Kaempferol, a flavonoid, helps alleviate shortness of breath by possessing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help improve lung function and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are commonly used due to their high concentration of medicinal compounds that help relieve respiratory issues.
Leaves: The leaves are often used in decoctions because they contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate shortness of breath and coughs.
Stems: The stems are sometimes used due to their ability to provide a soothing effect on the respiratory system, which can help alleviate shortness of breath and other respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest eupatorium perfoliatum flowers and leaves in the summer months when the plant is in full bloom.
Clean and dry the eupatorium perfoliatum flowers and leaves to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Combine 1-2 cups of dried eupatorium perfoliatum flowers and leaves with 4 cups of boiling water in a pot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes then strain the liquid to remove the plant material from the decoction.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and consume 1/2 cup 2-3 times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with shortness of breath because it relaxes the airways, reducing inflammation and constriction that can cause wheezing and shortness of breath. The menthol in peppermint also helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel and clearing out blocked airways. Additionally, peppermint's expectorant properties help to loosen and cough up phlegm, further alleviating congestion and reducing the sensation of shortness of breath. By promoting smooth airflow and clearing out obstructions, peppermint decoctions can provide rapid relief from respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Peppermint's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
Bronchodilatory effects: The menthol content in peppermint can help relax the smooth muscle in the airways, widening the bronchi and improving airflow, which reduces shortness of breath.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, may help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can contribute to breathing difficulties.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol acts as a bronchodilator, helping to relax airway muscles and increase airflow, thereby alleviating shortness of breath.

Menthanol: Menthanol exhibits expectorant properties, which facilitate the removal of mucus and other secretions from the airways, allowing for easier breathing.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the early morning.
Chop the fresh leaves and flowers into small pieces and crush them in a mortar.
Combine the crushed plant material with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes and then strain it through a cheesecloth into a separate container.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of lemon verbena decoctions may help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can contribute to shortness of breath and other respiratory issues.

Flavonoids (including verbascoside and verbenoside): These flavonoids possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to relax the airway muscles and improve oxygen flow to the lungs.

Terpenes (including citral and limonene): These terpenes have bronchodilatory effects, which means they help to open up airways, allowing for easier breathing and relieving shortness of breath symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain volatile oils and flavonoids that have been traditionally used to relieve respiratory issues, including shortness of breath.
Flowers: The flowers are used because they contain fragrant oils and flavonoids that help to relax the airways and alleviate breathing difficulties.
Roots: The roots are used because they contain compounds like verbascoside, which helps to relax the respiratory system and alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried verbena officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning when dew is still present.
Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried plant material or 2-3 teaspoons of fresh material.
Combine the measured plant material with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass or ceramic container.
Allow the decoction to steep for 5-10 minutes then strain it using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Store the cooled decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 fresh taraxacum officinale roots and clean them thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine them with 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the plant material.
Store the final decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days before consumption.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 fresh achillea millefolium flowers and leaves in the early morning or late afternoon.
Dry the harvested material in a warm well-ventilated area for 7-10 days.
Mix 2-3 teaspoons of dried flowers and leaves with 1 quart of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids to enjoy the herbal remedy.

remedy_constituents: Foeniculin: A sesquiterpene lactone found in Foeniculum vulgare, which helps alleviate shortness of breath by exhibiting expectorant properties, aiding in the removal of mucus from the airways.

remedy_recipe: Gather foeniculum vulgare seeds or roots in quantities of 2-4 teaspoons per cup of water.
Combine the gathered foeniculum vulgare with 1 quart of boiling water and let steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.
Store the strained decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours to preserve its potency.
Serve the cooled decoction in doses of 1/2 cup as needed to promote digestive health.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves or 1 cup dried material.
Rinse the harvested sambucus nigra material gently with cold running water.
Combine the rinsed sambucus nigra material with 4 cups of boiling distilled water.
Simmer the mixture for 15-20 minutes to allow for optimal extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried rosa rugosa flowers and leaves in the ratio of one part herbs to four parts water.
Combine the herb mixture with cold water in a heat-resistant container at a rate of 250 milliliters per gram of herbs.
Bring the mixture to a boil using high heat then reduce the heat to low for 30 minutes to allow for extraction.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool before refrigerating it and consuming it within three days in dosages of one cup twice daily.

remedy_recipe: Weigh out one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and add it to a clean glass jar.
Pour one cup of boiling water over the ginkgo biloba leaves and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large mug, discarding the solids.
Add honey to taste and stir well before consuming the decoction immediately after preparation.
Repeat the process twice daily as needed, taking at least an hour apart from meals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry decoctions helps with shortness of breath because it is rich in antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and expectorants. The decoction's properties help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion that can contribute to shortness of breath. This is because the berries contain anthraquinones, which stimulate the production of mucin, a thick, protective substance that helps expel mucus.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Highbush cranberry decoctions have anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and lungs, making it easier to breathe. This can be especially helpful for people with conditions like bronchitis or chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD).

remedy_recipe: Rinse one teaspoon of dried vaccinium macrocarpon berries with cold water to remove impurities.
Combine the rinsed berries with two cups of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant glass or ceramic container.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 15-20 minutes, allowing flavors and nutrients to infuse.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding solids.
Store the cooled decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain decoctions helps with shortness of breath because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and relax the airways, reducing constriction and allowing for easier breathing. The herb's expectorant properties also help to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm, further alleviating congestion and discomfort. Additionally, plantain decoctions may help to calm the nervous system, which can contribute to shallow or rapid breathing patterns that exacerbate shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Plantain decoctions help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe by thinning out congestion and phlegm.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the lungs and airways, which can be caused by respiratory conditions such as bronchitis or asthma, allowing for easier breathing.
Antispasmodic properties: Plantain's antispasmodic effects help to relax the airway muscles, reducing spasms that can cause wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including shortness of breath, due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Barks: The barks of the plantain plant contain flavonoids and alkaloids that help to relax bronchial muscles, alleviating congestion and shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean 20g of dried plantago major leaves and flowers at dawn.
Measure 10g of the herb and add it to a stainless steel pot filled with 500ml of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 15-20 minutes then strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Bring the strained liquid to a rolling boil in a new pot and reduce heat to simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Allow the decoction to cool before transferring it to dark glass bottles and storing them in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea decoctions helps with shortness of breath because they have a calming effect on the respiratory system, relaxing bronchial tubes and improving airflow. The antioxidants present in herbal teas also help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the warming properties of some herbal teas can help to loosen and clear out excess mucus, further reducing congestion and shortness of breath. Overall, drinking herbal tea decoctions can provide natural relief from respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Herbs like thyme, eucalyptus, and marshmallow root have expectorant properties, which means they help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal teas rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as peppermint, ginger, and licorice root, can help reduce inflammation in the airways and lungs, making breathing easier.

Bronchodilator properties: Herbs like fenugreek, turmeric, and ginkgo biloba have bronchodilator effects, which means they help relax the airway muscles and increase airflow to the lungs, improving oxygenation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, the roots help to relax bronchial spasms and reduce inflammation in the airways, making it effective for alleviating shortness of breath.
Barks: The bark contains a compound called theaflavin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm respiratory issues, including shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried camellia sinensis leaves and flowers per cup of water.
Bring the measured ingredients to a boil in a small saucepan over medium-high heat.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool slightly before drinking immediately while still warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with shortness of breath because it contains potent anti-inflammatory compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, which effectively alleviate respiratory tract congestion. The decoction's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, reducing bronchial constriction and opening up airways, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to soothe and protect lung tissues, further enhancing its respiratory benefits. As a result, pot marigold decoctions have been traditionally used to treat various respiratory issues, including shortness of breath, coughs, and bronchitis.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Pot marigold decoction helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe by promoting expectoration.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in pot marigold may help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to shortness of breath by relaxing constricted bronchial tubes.
Antispasmodic properties: Pot marigold's antispasmodic effects can help relax and calm spasms in the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing and coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce bronchial constriction and alleviate respiratory distress.
Flowers: Antioxidant and antispasmodic compounds in the flowers soothe bronchial tubes, reducing inflammation and constriction.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried calendula officinalis flowers.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine one teaspoon of the powdered calendula with one cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the mixture to infuse and cool slightly.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to shortness of breath that are treated with herbal decoctions are respiratory conditions such as bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). Herbal decoctions made from herbs like ginger, turmeric, and licorice root can help alleviate symptoms of these conditions by reducing inflammation, relaxing airways, and improving lung function. These decoctions may also be used to treat other respiratory issues, such as bronchial spasms, wheezing, and chest congestion.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with shortness of breath is a blend of ginseng, eleuthero, and licorice root. Ginseng helps to improve lung function and increase oxygenation, while eleuthero enhances overall respiratory health and reduces inflammation. Licorice root soothes and calms the respiratory system, reducing congestion and promoting relaxation. Drinking these decoctions together can help alleviate shortness of breath and improve overall respiratory well-being.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by The European respiratory journal, herbal decoctions may be beneficial for individuals experiencing shortness of breath due to chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). The study, which systematically assessed the effectiveness of 14 different herbal medicines, found significant intergroup differences for one or more outcomes in several herbal medicines, including Panax ginseng and Salvia miltiorrhiza. While the effectiveness of herbal medicines for treating COPD is not established beyond reasonable doubt, these results suggest that certain herbal decoctions may help alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath in patients with COPD.
